cityDNA.coreFantasy: A wood elves city where Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove, Religious / Temple Quarter, Records / Archives, Arcane / Alchemical Labs, University / Lyceum, and Wood-Elven Heartroot Cathedral of Living Branches turn forest, root network, old growth woodland, dry ground and oak, limestone blocks, timber, arcane glow, antler into a distinct civic machine.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: Thalewood should feel grown rather than built: a civic forest where every archive, temple, and school bends around living roots.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: Thalewood organizes public life around patient stewardship: its districts convert Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove, Religious / Temple Quarter, Records / Archives, Arcane / Alchemical Labs, University / Lyceum into a readable system of authority, labor, memory, and movement.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: Thalewood should be recognized by Oak, Limestone Blocks, Timber, Arcane Glow, Antler, Forest, Root Network, Old Growth Woodland, Dry Ground, Ward Networks, Ritual Geometry, Moonlight Refraction, Celestial Projection, Living Roots, and the named anchors Wood-Elven Heartroot Cathedral of Living Branches, Sacred Grove Beacon, and Star Map Ceilings Veiled High Court.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: The city belongs to wood elves; its civic habits should grow from the sidecar-backed district spine rather than generic fantasy urbanism.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: Wood-Elven Heartroot Cathedral of Living Branches, Sacred Grove Beacon, Star Map Ceilings Veiled High Court, and the five section landmark families symbolize patient stewardship while warning against ancient sanctity becoming exclusion or inertia.

canonLocks: Thalewood is a wood elves city, not a generic settlement with cosmetic motifs.
The Wonder is Wood-Elven Heartroot Cathedral of Living Branches.
The Beacon is Sacred Grove Beacon.
The Seat of Power is Star Map Ceilings Veiled High Court, located at the District 13 authority center.
The five district sections are Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove, Religious / Temple Quarter, Records / Archives, Arcane / Alchemical Labs, University / Lyceum.
The board should be treated as twenty-five district slots grouped into five section identities.
Section 1 (Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove) preserves these exact anchor names: Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove Civic Hall, Sacred Grove Beacon, Hanging Gardens Monument, Living Canopy Hall Nook.
Section 2 (Religious / Temple Quarter) preserves these exact anchor names: Religious / Temple Quarter Civic Hall, Temple Platform, Religious Temple Quarter Lantern Monument, Procession Stair Nook.
Section 3 (Records / Archives) preserves these exact anchor names: Records / Archives Hall, Archive Tower, Records Archives Gold-Seal Monument, Copyist Hall Nook.
Section 4 (Arcane / Alchemical Labs) preserves these exact anchor names: Arcane / Alchemical Labs Civic Hall, Alchemy Lab, Blue-Lamp Monument, Formula Archive Nook.
Section 5 (University / Lyceum) preserves these exact anchor names: University / Lyceum Civic Hall, Lecture Hall, Star Navigation Monument, Observatory Court Nook.
provisionalCanon: Individual district-slot names beyond exact sidecar anchors are organizational labels for the 25-seat board, not new landmark canon.
Minor street customs should be derived from the section spine unless future source files provide more exact anchors.
rejectedReads: Thalewood as a generic fantasy city without its named Wonder, Beacon, and Seat of Power.
Replacing exact sidecar anchor names with generic labels such as market, temple, tower, wall, or archive.
Treating district section labels as decorative instead of civic systems that drive scenes and assets.
cautionFlags: Preserve exact named anchors from sidecars.
Use the culture identity respectfully as civic design language rather than costume.
Keep all 25 district slots legible even when five slots share one section identity.
Do not add unsupported individual district landmark names beyond the source-backed anchors.

environment.environmentalLogic: Thalewood's environment should make Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove, Religious / Temple Quarter, Records / Archives, Arcane / Alchemical Labs, University / Lyceum feel physically necessary. Terrain, light, civic routes, and landmark placement must support movement through all five section identities rather than read as interchangeable scenery.

skyline: Wood-Elven Heartroot Cathedral of Living Branches is the wonder-scale skyline identifier.
Star Map Ceilings Veiled High Court marks the central authority mass at District 13.
Sacred Grove Beacon must remain visible as the public signal landmark.
Section landmarks should form a readable ring of Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove, Religious / Temple Quarter, Records / Archives, Arcane / Alchemical Labs, University / Lyceum.
infrastructure: Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove Civic Hall

civilization.people: Thalewood's people are wood elves; they should be written through shared civic habits, public spaces, work rhythms, and the visible demands of the five section identities.
socialClasses: officials and stewards tied to Star Map Ceilings Veiled High Court
landmark keepers and civic hall administrators
street vendors, porters, runners, and maintenance crews
families living around the section-specific local anchors
civilization.governance: Authority centers on Star Map Ceilings Veiled High Court and is made visible through section civic halls, named landmarks, permissions, public signals, and the citywide response to Sacred Grove Beacon.
civilization.religion: Religious or ceremonial life should be shown through source-backed shrines, temples, wonders, oath spaces, ancestor sites, or ritual geometry when those appear in the district spine.
customs: citizens orient themselves by the section identity of a street before they identify an address
public notices and signals are interpreted through the Beacon and civic halls
named landmarks act as meeting points, proof points, and local memory anchors
section-specific work routines shape food, clothing, posture, and street traffic
District 13 authority matters even when scenes occur in outer district slots
bodyLanguage: citizens check landmarks and signals before crossing into another section
officials use the posture and tools of their section role
workers treat section anchors as practical wayfinding points
crowds become more formal near the Seat of Power and more local near minor landmarks
speechPatterns: directions use exact landmark names rather than generic descriptions
official speech references permits, seals, routes, oaths, or signals as appropriate to the city
working speech is concrete, location-heavy, and tied to civic function
locals distinguish between the Wonder, Beacon, Seat, and section landmarks without confusion

streets.appearance: Street scenes should make Thalewood immediately identifiable through Oak, Limestone Blocks, Timber, Arcane Glow, Antler, Forest, Root Network, Old Growth Woodland, Dry Ground, Ward Networks, Ritual Geometry, Moonlight Refraction, Celestial Projection, Living Roots, and the exact section anchors rather than generic fantasy facades.
streets.movement: Movement flows through five section identities and twenty-five district slots. People navigate by Sacred Grove Beacon, Star Map Ceilings Veiled High Court, section civic halls, major landmarks, monuments, and minor nooks.
streets.lighting: Lighting should use Mist Veils, White Limestone Civic Blocks, Lantern Lit, Civic Blue Glow, Sun Disc Reliefs, symbolic civic object, with the Beacon and Seat carrying distinct civic emphasis.
streets.weatherEffects: Weather should interact with forest, root network, old growth woodland, dry ground and the city's source-backed materials, altering visibility, travel speed, public gathering, and signal readability.
streets.dayCycle: Daytime emphasizes section labor: Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove, Religious / Temple Quarter, Records / Archives, Arcane / Alchemical Labs, University / Lyceum. Civic halls, shops, and public works create the main public rhythm.
streets.nightCycle: Night scenes should preserve the city's identity through landmark silhouettes, beacon light, watch routes, late shops, ritual spaces, and section-specific sounds.

common: nature preserve / sacred grove supply stalls
uncommon: nature preserve / sacred grove specialist counters
prestigious: workshops or offices near Star Map Ceilings Veiled High Court
specialist houses serving Wood-Elven Heartroot Cathedral of Living Branches
master artisans tied to section landmarks

housing.wealthy: High-status housing clusters near Star Map Ceilings Veiled High Court, major terraces, and prestigious landmark approaches; it should display the city materials cleanly and control sightlines toward Wonder or Beacon.
housing.middle: Middle housing occupies upper rooms, side courts, work-family compounds, and mixed-use streets close to civic halls and section services.
housing.poor: Poorer housing should be practical and specific to local labor, with patched materials, shared courtyards, crowded lanes, and strong dependence on nearby section anchors.

wonder.name: Wood-Elven Heartroot Cathedral of Living Branches
wonder.symbolicRole: The citywide symbol of patient stewardship made monumental.
wonder.visualRole: A wonder-scale skyline anchor using Oak, Limestone Blocks, Timber, Arcane Glow, Antler, Forest, Root Network, Old Growth Woodland, Dry Ground, and the wood elves visual language.
wonder.civicRole: Represents the whole city above the five sections: Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove, Religious / Temple Quarter, Records / Archives, Arcane / Alchemical Labs, University / Lyceum.
storyHooks: access to Wood-Elven Heartroot Cathedral of Living Branches changes public legitimacy
a citywide ritual reveals hidden pressure
the Wonder becomes a navigation or warning reference

beacon.name: Sacred Grove Beacon
beacon.symbolicRole: Public signal and shared alarm for Thalewood.
beacon.visualRole: A section-scale signal landmark that must read immediately as the city Beacon.
beacon.civicRole: Coordinates public response, civic warnings, and Conjunction-state attention.
storyHooks: someone controls the first interpretation of a signal
the Beacon redirects traffic between sections
a warning exposes which citizens trust civic authority

districtDioramaGuidance: Create twenty-five district dioramas grouped into five recognizable sections: Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove, Religious / Temple Quarter, Records / Archives, Arcane / Alchemical Labs, University / Lyceum.
Each district should use its districtNumber and sectionNumber, but avoid baked text labels in imagery.
D13 must read as the Seat of Power district centered on Star Map Ceilings Veiled High Court.
Repeated section anchors may appear across five related slots, but each slot needs its own street function, crowd density, and prop emphasis.
Use exact source-backed anchor names for file naming, prompt references, and canon cross-checking.
S1 must preserve Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove with anchors: Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove Civic Hall, Sacred Grove Beacon, Hanging Gardens Monument, Living Canopy Hall Nook.
S2 must preserve Religious / Temple Quarter with anchors: Religious / Temple Quarter Civic Hall, Temple Platform, Religious Temple Quarter Lantern Monument, Procession Stair Nook.
S3 must preserve Records / Archives with anchors: Records / Archives Hall, Archive Tower, Records Archives Gold-Seal Monument, Copyist Hall Nook.
S4 must preserve Arcane / Alchemical Labs with anchors: Arcane / Alchemical Labs Civic Hall, Alchemy Lab, Blue-Lamp Monument, Formula Archive Nook.
S5 must preserve University / Lyceum with anchors: University / Lyceum Civic Hall, Lecture Hall, Star Navigation Monument, Observatory Court Nook.
mapBackgroundGuidance: Map backgrounds should keep Wood-Elven Heartroot Cathedral of Living Branches as the skyline symbol, Star Map Ceilings Veiled High Court at the central civic authority, and Sacred Grove Beacon as the readable public signal.

Arrange the five section identities so Thalewood does not converge with other cities: Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove, Religious / Temple Quarter, Records / Archives, Arcane / Alchemical Labs, University / Lyceum.

Use terrain cues (Forest, Root Network, Old Growth Woodland, Dry Ground), material cues (Oak, Limestone Blocks, Timber, Arcane Glow, Antler), and magic cues (Ward Networks, Ritual Geometry, Moonlight Refraction, Celestial Projection, Living Roots) consistently across district clusters.
Show roads, terraces, gates, courts, or paths that make the twenty-five-district tactical layout plausible.
requiredVisualElements: Wood-Elven Heartroot Cathedral of Living Branches
Star Map Ceilings Veiled High Court
elementsToAvoid: extra top-level schema sections or embedded generation notes
generic fantasy city assets that ignore exact anchor names
unlabeled invented landmarks replacing sidecar names
flattening five sections into one visual district
treating culture identity as costume rather than civic design
overcrowded detail that hides Wonder, Beacon, or Seat of Power
